name: Oscar Hammerstein II
description: American librettist, lyricist, theatrical producer, and director of musicals (1895–1960)
abstract: Oscar Greeley Clendenning Hammerstein II (; July 12, 1895 – August 23, 1960) was an American lyricist, librettist, theatrical producer, and director in musical theater for nearly 40 years. He won eight Tony Awards and two Academy Awards for Best Original Song.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: Mr. Hammerstein and Sir Alfred Butt and Jerome Kern said they wanted the real thing... they had found it in me. So that's how I got the job.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I always thought – I was always an English major... I begin to – as I begin to become a lyric writer and admire all the great lyricists – W. S. Gilbert and people like that, Oscar Hammerstein the Second – I really love him
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Oscar Hammerstein and, “Ol’ Man River,” Jerome Kerns, “Ol’ Man River,” and Dorothy Hammerstein got furious she said, “Mr. Kern wrote da-da-dada, Oscar wrote, “Ol’ Man River.””
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: we had a man in Harlem at one time Oscar Hammerstein, was like an official host... he became quite an official host, you know, I mean like an official greeter for the city

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Oscar Hammerstein’s wife Dorothy was here with some people, and they were saying something about her husband wrote Ol’ Man River... Oscar Hammerstein.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
